calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Proof Of Transfer.
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Off Calendar

Petition To Transfer Venue - Probate Code 17005(A)(1)
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carrol Collins for (trustee) Northern Trust.

The Court asked counsel to explain why there was no appearance on 2/29/16 & 3/21/16. Mr. Collins reported that he was not aware of the February hearing and gave a history on what had transpired prior to that date, that he did not appear for the March hearing as he had filed his petition in Los Angeles and notified the probate examiner and thought that was the end of it, that in August he was told by the Los Angeles Court that he needed to file his petition in San Francisco which he did. Mr. Collins further reported that Ms. Glaze has recently married, gave a history of how the trust came to be, that Ms. Glaze has managed to save $200,000.00 which shows that she is able to manage her own funds, that they would like to dismiss the petition for change of venue and instead file a petition to waive further accountings, that in 2008 when the conservatorship terminated it was thought that court supervision was a good idea but that they currently have an estate plan in place that will not require the Court to be involved. The Court confirmed that the administration is still done in Los Angeles. Mr. Collins stated that if a waiver of accountings is granted there will be no need to transfer the case as it will not be necessary to go back to court, that the only issue would be the terms of the trust and that San Francisco has had this trust and conservatorship for 30 years. The Court stated that it will take the petition to transfer venue under submission, reminded counsel that on 3/21/16 the Court suspended the trustee and that it will need to modify the order to reinstate him. Mr. Carroll apologized, stating that it was his own fault and not the fault of Northern Trust. Mr. Carroll further stated that in 2012 the Court had the same jurisdiction concerns but said it was okay and that Ms. Glaze moves frequently and it is easier to keep everything here. The Court confirmed with counsel that the basis for his request is that if there are no accountings there will be no further action in this court. Mr. Collins responded that the accountings were required for the transition from the conservatorship, that Ms. Glaze is now married and has proven that she can handle her finances. The Court took the petition to transfer venue under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Filing Petition For Change Of Venue.
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kinnear. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Filing Petition For Change Of Venue.
Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Carroll Collins to appear on March 21, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the petition for change of venue has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Filing Petition For Change Of Venue.
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Continued To Feb-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carroll Collins for Northern Trust.

Mr. Carroll stated that he had submitted an order to the court from the hearing in December, and that the order was never signed. The court stated that said order is not in the file and counsel provided a copy. Mr. Carroll further stated that he renews his request that the court reconsider its direction for change of venue, that the trust has been in this court for many years, that they only come in every two years and that it is beneficial for it to remain here. The Court stated that another Judicial Officer had made the ruling in December and that this Judicial Officer is not inclined to change it. Mr. Carroll stated that Ms. Glaze wants to change the terms of the trust, that it will require court authorization, that it would be easier to do so in this court, that she is allowed to amend the trust and that they will need to file a petition. The status on filing a petition for change of venue was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carroll Collins for petitioner Northern Trust Bank of California

The Court stated that he was prepared to grant the petition for approval of the accounting, and to set a status hearing re filing a petition to change venue to Southern California. Mr. Carroll stated that the matter has been before the San Francisco Superior Court since 1988, first as a conservatorship and since 2008 as a trust. He contended that the petitioner has offices throughout California, and that the duties of administration were chiefly making entries on computers, which could be done with equal effectiveness anywhere in the state, so there was no need for a change of venue and the increased costs which would attend the filing of a petition and changing venue. The Court responded that venue is appropriate where the trust is being administered, and that bit had come to the Court's attention that this trust was being administered in Southern California. The Court set a status hearing re filing a petition for change of venue for January 25, 2016, at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Court granted the petition approving the accounting (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not reported)
